Utilities for truncating assertion output.
Current default behaviour is to truncate assertion explanations at
~8 terminal lines, unless running in "-vv" mode or running on CI.
List)
Optional)
Item
use '-vv' to show)
explanation
item
max_length
returnc
zGTruncate this assertion explanation if the given test item is eligible.)
_should_truncate_item
_truncate_explanation)
>/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/_pytest/assertion/truncate.py
truncate_if_required
z9Whether or not this test item is eligible for truncation.

option
verbose
_running_on_ci)
z0Check if we're currently running on a CI system.Z
BUILD_NUMBERc
environ)
varr
	<genexpr>$
z!_running_on_ci.<locals>.<genexpr>)
any)
env_varsr
input_lines
	max_lines
	max_charsr	
Truncate given list of strings that makes up the assertion explanation.
    Truncates to either 8 lines, or 640 characters - whichever the input reaches
    first. The remaining lines will be replaced by a usage message.
    N
...Full output truncatedz
 line hidden)z
 lines hidden)z
DEFAULT_MAX_LINES
DEFAULT_MAX_CHARS

_truncate_by_char_count
	USAGE_MSG
